etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree") + phonetic 才(才) (cái, "talent (phonetic)") -> material; timber
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 木(木) (mù, "tree") as the semantic component, representing trees or wood, with 才(才) (cái), which provides the phonetic element. The combination indicates timber as processed wood material, derived from trees, and this extends metaphorically to 'material' in general as a resource or substance that can be worked with, much like wood.

examples.chinese: 这座房子是用优质的木材建造的(這座房子是用優質的木材建造的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zuò fángzi shì yòng yōuzhì de mùcái jiànzào de.
examples.english: This house is built with high-quality timber.

examples.chinese: 他具备成为优秀工程师的材料(他具備成為優秀工程師的材料)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jùbèi chéngwéi yōuxiù gōngchéngshī de cáiliào.
examples.english: He has the material to become an excellent engineer.
